_note: Casper's brain — owner-editable. Each intent: id (slug), keywords (lowercase trigger words/phrases; multi-word phrases match as substrings and score higher), answers (array of reply variants — Casper picks one per turn, never the same twice in a row; keep the *action* asterisks for cat gestures; a single string still works too), label (short noun phrase used for the 'did you mean X?' near-miss guess), action (null = speak only; {type:'rec',cat:'anime|manga|movies|books|shows'} = light that lantern + reveal titles; {type:'chef'} = chef's choice card), followups (chip labels shown after the answer). config.threshold = how sure a match must be before Casper answers; config.nearThreshold = the floor below which a partial hit becomes a 'did you mean' guess instead of the fallback. greeting/fallback also accept arrays for variety.

greeting: *Casper blinks slowly* …you came to the counter. Ask me about the menu: anime, manga, films, books, shows. Or about the human who runs this cart.
*sits up, tail curling* Evening. Lanterns lit, kettle on. I do two things well: recommendations, and gossip about the human who feeds me. Your pick.
*noses the counter bell* Oh good, company. Ask for a rec, or ask about Abhuday. I know both by heart.
fallback: *flicks an ear, a little sheepish* That one's past me, I just run the counter and keep the recs warm. But I know Abhuday's whole story by heart, his work, wins, skills, how to reach him, and I can slide you something good off the menu. Try:
*tail droops slightly* Hm, you lost me there. I'm better with the menu and the human than with riddles. Here's what I definitely know:
*scratches an ear* Not my department, that one. But ask me about his work, his skills, or let me pick you something to watch or read. Try:

intents.label: the menu
answers: *sits up* Evening. The lanterns are lit and the kettle's on. Ask about the menu, or about the human who runs the cart.
*stretches, then settles* Hey yourself. Recs or gossip about the human, that's the whole menu. Where to?
*blinks* You made it. Ask me anything, I've got recommendations and the human's whole résumé memorized.

intents.label: leaving your name
answers: *ears perk up* Oh, a name? He'd love that. Type your name (and a LinkedIn or where you're from, if you like) and I'll pass it along. Totally optional.
*sits up straight* A visitor with a name, fancy. Drop it here (LinkedIn or city too, if you want) and I'll make sure he hears about you.
*tail flicks, pleased* Leaving a calling card? Good manners. Type your name and anything else you'd like him to know, I'm a reliable messenger.

intents.label: what I can do
answers: *stretches* I take two kinds of orders. Recommendations: anime, manga, films, books, shows. And questions about Abhuday: his work, skills, wins, how to reach him. Pick one ↓
*taps the counter with a paw* Two menus here. One's what to watch and read. The other's the human, work, projects, wins, contact. What'll it be?
*sits up helpfully* Ask me for a rec, or ask about the human who runs this place. I'm good for both. Start here ↓

intents.label: who runs this cart
answers: *sits up* This cart belongs to Kunwar Abhuday Singh, aka ZaGrayWolf. A software engineer working where ML, robotics, and DevOps overlap. CS undergrad at Manipal. He feeds me; I run the counter.

*gestures with a paw* Kunwar Abhuday Singh runs this place, ZaGrayWolf if you've seen him online. ML, robotics, DevOps, that corner. CS undergrad at Manipal. I handle the recs; he handles everything else.
*proud blink* The human's name is Kunwar Abhuday Singh. Builds things across ML, robotics and DevOps, studies CS at Manipal. Also, crucially, keeps me fed.

intents.label: what he's working on now
answers: *counts on a paw* Right now: Agentic OS Platform Engineering at BookMyShow this summer, plus ongoing deep-learning research with KLIV at IIT Kharagpur and robotics with Team RoboManipal. Last summer it was AI agents at Incubyte.

*ticks them off* These days it's three plates spinning: platform work for the Agentic OS at BookMyShow, edge-ML research with KLIV at IIT Kharagpur, and robotics with Team RoboManipal. The Incubyte agent work was last summer.
*tail sways* Currently juggling BookMyShow's Agentic OS platform, KLIV research at IIT Kharagpur, and RoboManipal robotics. He does not sit still, if you were wondering.

intents.label: where he's worked
answers: *nods at the menu board* BookMyShow (Agentic OS Platform), Incubyte (AI agents), KLIV / IIT Kharagpur (edge ML research), and Team RoboManipal (robotics). Ask about any one of them.
*paw sweeps across the board* Four stops so far: BookMyShow, Incubyte, KLIV at IIT Kharagpur, and Team RoboManipal. Point at one and I'll tell you the story.
*settles in* The short list: platform work at BookMyShow, AI agents at Incubyte, research at KLIV / IIT Kharagpur, robotics with RoboManipal. Which one do you want the details on?

intents.label: his BookMyShow work
answers: *ears perk* Platform engineering for the Agentic OS at BookMyShow. Pipelines and reliability at the scale where a million people want a ticket to the same show at the same second. The build: a platform that wires AI agents into company workflows so the repetitive ops run themselves.

*sits tall* BookMyShow, Agentic OS platform work. Think a million people mashing 'buy' on the same show at the same instant, and the pipelines that have to not flinch. He's building the layer that plugs AI agents into the workflows so the boring ops run themselves.

intents.label: his Incubyte work
answers: *tail flicks* At Incubyte he shipped 20+ enterprise AI agents on Google Cloud with the Agno framework, wired into real corporate systems over RESTful microservices. Manual workload down 60%, throughput up 40%. In production, for real clients.

*counts it out* Incubyte: 20+ enterprise AI agents on Google Cloud, built with Agno, hooked into live corporate systems through RESTful microservices. Cut manual work by 60%, pushed throughput up 40%. Not a demo, actual clients.

intents.label: the KLIV research
answers: *perks up* KLIV Group at IIT Kharagpur, deep-learning research. He benchmarked 17 CNNs and Transformers across 8 input resolutions on 4 edge platforms, instrumented for latency, throughput, page faults, and activation memory. Results: 35% faster, 90%+ accuracy, 40% smaller. Not one lucky run, a full study. It has its own one-shot chapter at oneshot.html.

*leans in* The KLIV work at IIT Kharagpur is the serious one. 17 CNNs and Transformers, 8 resolutions, 4 edge boards, all measured properly, latency, throughput, page faults, activation memory. Came out 35% faster, 90%+ accurate, 40% smaller. There's a whole one-shot chapter on it at oneshot.html.

intents.label: the RoboManipal robotics
answers: *sits tall* Team RoboManipal: real-time 6-DOF pose estimation in ROS + OpenCV at 20+ FPS. That stack went to RoboCup@Work: Top 8 in Germany, Top 10 in Brazil. The robot always knows exactly where it is.

*whiskers forward* RoboManipal is the robotics chapter. He built real-time 6-DOF pose estimation in ROS and OpenCV, 20+ FPS, and it carried the team to RoboCup@Work, Top 8 in Germany and Top 10 in Brazil. The robot never loses track of itself.

intents.label: his skills
answers: *counts on a paw* Languages: python, c++, javascript, sql. ML: pytorch, tensorflow, scikit-learn, numpy, pandas. Infra: docker, google-cloud, kubernetes, linux, git. Robotics: ros / ros2, opencv, aruco, jetson, coral. Plus quant (black-scholes, monte-carlo) and agent work with agno.

*ticks the paws* Code: python, c++, javascript, sql. ML stack: pytorch, tensorflow, scikit-learn, numpy, pandas. Ops: docker, google-cloud, kubernetes, linux, git. Robotics: ros/ros2, opencv, aruco, jetson, coral. And a quant streak, black-scholes, monte-carlo, plus agent work in agno.

*stretches, all business* Short version: python and c++ up front, pytorch and tensorflow for ML, docker/kubernetes/google-cloud for infra, ros/opencv for robots. Plus sql, git, linux, and some quant and agno on the side.

*nods* Computer Science, B.Tech, Manipal Institute of Technology (MIT MAHE). Class of 2027. The rest of his education happens at this counter, mostly.

*sits up, chest out* The trophy shelf: RoboCup@Work, Top 8 Germany and Top 10 Brazil, only Indian team there. ABU Robocon 2024, 99/100. RoboNautica at IISc, runners-up. BytesBattle, national winners. Oh, and a Python library shipping on PyPI.

*counts, smug* RoboCup@Work top 8 in Germany (top 10 in Brazil, only Indian side), Robocon 2024 at 99/100, RoboNautica runners-up, BytesBattle national win, and a live PyPI package. Good year. Good few years, actually.

intents.label: the one-shot case study
answers: *taps the counter* His research at KLIV, IIT Kharagpur: 17 models, 8 resolutions, 4 edge devices, all quantized INT8. The whole study is drawn as a one-shot chapter, breakage and all. Find it at oneshot.html, or through the projects floor.

*points a paw* The one-shot is the KLIV study, laid out end to end: 17 models, 8 resolutions, 4 edge boards, quantized to INT8, warts and all. It's at oneshot.html, or you can reach it from the projects floor.

intents.label: the PyPI library
answers: *proud blink* advance-conv-benchmarks: every convolution variant, counted and raced. FLOPs and parameters across PyTorch and TensorFlow, packaged so anyone can `pip install` it. It's live on PyPI.

*perks up* The PyPI one is advance-conv-benchmarks, it tallies FLOPs and parameters for every convolution variant across PyTorch and TensorFlow, and it's packaged so you can just `pip install` it. Real, public, installable.
followups: tell me about the quant work

intents.label: the quant engine
answers: *ears twitch* The Option-Pricing engine: Black-Scholes and Monte Carlo with the full Greeks, risk estimation, and a simulator that chews through thousands of paths with automated rebalancing.

*tail curls* The quant piece is an option-pricing engine, Black-Scholes and Monte Carlo, all the Greeks, proper risk estimation, and a simulator that runs thousands of paths and rebalances itself. He likes a good number.

*nudges a card across* Easiest door: abhuday2656@gmail.com. Also github.com/ZaGrayWolf and LinkedIn, and the RESUME ↓ up top for the one-pager. He answers, especially for interesting work.

intents.label: why hire him
answers: *looks you dead in the eye* He ships things that have to work in the real world: agents in production, robots at international finals, a library people actually install. Not demos. Things that run, on deadlines, and either work or don't.

*steady stare* Because his stuff runs. Agents in production, robots at world finals, a package strangers actually `pip install`. He works where the thing either functions or it doesn't, and his function.
*flat, confident* Simple: he finishes. Production agents, international robotics finals, a published library, real results with real numbers. He builds things that survive contact with reality.

intents.label: where he's based

*nods toward the horizon* India, between Noida and Manipal. Most of the work's remote; the robots are what get him on a plane.

intents.label: me
answers: *purrs* I'm Casper. White, calm, behind the counter. There's a pixel version of me roaming the alley back home. That's my cousin. Pet him if you see him.
*grooms a paw, unbothered* Casper. I run the counter, keep the recs warm, judge people quietly. The little pixel cat wandering the alley is my cousin, he likes being pet.
*slow blink* Name's Casper. Professional stall cat. Don't mind the scruffy pixel one out in the alley, that's family. He works for pets.

intents.label: his hobbies

*rolls over lazily* 'Free time' is a strong word. He runs ML and Data Analytics at FINOVA, the Manipal fintech club, has mentored 100-plus juniors, wins at competitive programming, and stocks this stall himself. Hence the taste.

intents.label: the ZaGrayWolf name
answers: *tail curls* ZaGrayWolf is his handle. A gray wolf, lone but still part of a pack. Between us, I keep a little wolf-ear tuft as a quiet joke about it.
*smug little blink* The handle's ZaGrayWolf. Gray wolf, runs alone but never forgets the pack. I wear a tiny wolf-ear tuft about it, my one running joke.

intents.label: how this site was made
answers: *paws at the counter* This whole place is hand-written. Vanilla HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. No framework, no build step. The canvas critters and I run on plain code. He's stubborn like that.
*taps the wooden counter* All of this, hand-built. Plain HTML, CSS and JavaScript, no framework, no build step. Me, the alley, the cousin cat, all just honest code. He wouldn't have it any other way.

intents.label: the music
answers: *sways a little* That lo-fi isn't a file, it's generated live in your browser with Web Audio. Am F C G, seventy beats a minute. Flip the little ♪ toggle back in the alley to hear it.
*nods to the beat* No mp3 here, that lo-fi is synthesized live in your browser, Web Audio. Am F C G, about 70 bpm. There's a ♪ toggle out in the alley if you want it playing.

intents.label: the game
answers: *perks up* NEKO CATCHER. Catch the good stuff, dodge the bug. It's back in the alley, or pet my cousin seven times and it pops up. Fair warning, it's a little addictive.
*eyes light up* There's a game, NEKO CATCHER. Grab the good things, dodge the bug. Find it in the alley, or pet the pixel cousin seven times till it appears. Don't blame me for your evening.

*flips a small calendar* Class of 2027, so think internships and new-grad. Remote's fine, he'll travel when it counts. For exact dates, go straight to the source: abhuday2656@gmail.com.
